11: fn cleanup_candidate_branches(
12:     trunk: &str,
13:     managed_branches: &[String],
14:     local_branches: &[String],
15: ) -> Vec<String> {
16:     let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();
17:     let mut branches = Vec::new();
18: 
19:     for branch in managed_branches {
20:         if branch != trunk && seen.insert(branch.clone()) {
21:             branches.push(branch.clone());
22:         }
23:     }
24: 
25:     for branch in local_branches {
26:         if branch != trunk && seen.insert(branch.clone()) {
27:             branches.push(branch.clone());
28:         }
29:     }
30: 
31:     branches
32: }
33: 
34: fn cleanup_reason(
35:     pr_info: Option<&github::PrInfo>,
36:     merged_via_git: bool,
37:     merged_via_diff: bool,
38: ) -> Option<&'static str> {
39:     if let Some(pr) = pr_info {
40:         if pr.merged {
41:             Some("merged")
42:         } else if pr.state == "CLOSED" {
43:             Some("pr_closed")
44:         } else {
45:             None
46:         }
47:     } else if merged_via_git || merged_via_diff {
48:         Some("merged")
49:     } else {
50:         None
51:     }
52: }
53: 
54: fn inside_worktree_path(current_dir: &str, worktree_path: &str) -> bool {
55:     fn normalize(path: &str) -> std::path::PathBuf {
56:         std::fs::canonicalize(path).unwrap_or_else(|_| std::path::PathBuf::from(path))
57:     }
58: 
59:     let current = normalize(current_dir);
60:     let worktree = normalize(worktree_path);
61:     current == worktree || current.starts_with(&worktree)
62: }
63: 
64: pub fn run(dry_run: bool, autostash: bool, force: bool) -> Result<()> {
65:     let state = StackState::load()?;
66:     if let Some(root) = git::current_linked_worktree_root()? {
67:         ui::linked_worktree_warning(&root);
68:     }
69: 
70:     if dry_run {
71:         ui::header("Sync preview (--dry-run, no changes will be made)");
72:         ui::info(&format!("Would fetch from `{}`", state.remote));
73:         ui::info(&format!(
74:             "Would update `{}` to latest remote (no checkout needed)",
75:             state.trunk
76:         ));
77: 
78:         let dry_worktree_map: std::collections::HashMap<String, String> = git::worktree_list()
79:             .unwrap_or_default()
80:             .into_iter()
81:             .filter(|wt| wt.path.contains("/.worktrees/"))
82:             .filter_map(|wt| wt.branch.map(|b| (b, wt.path)))
83:             .collect();
84: 
85:         let managed_branches: Vec<String> = state.branches.keys().cloned().collect();
86:         for branch_name in &managed_branches {
87:             let meta = state.get_branch(branch_name)?;
88:             if meta.pr_number.is_some() {
89:                 ui::info(&format!(
90:                     "Would check if PR for `{branch_name}` is merged or closed"
91:                 ));
92:                 if let Some(wt_path) = dry_worktree_map.get(branch_name.as_str()) {
93:                     ui::info(&format!("  → Would remove worktree at `{wt_path}`"));
94:                 }
95:             }
96:         }
97: 
98:         let order = state.topo_order();
99:         let mut any_restack = false;
100:         for branch_name in &order {
101:             let meta = state.get_branch(branch_name)?;
102:             let parent = &meta.parent;
103:             let stored_head = &meta.parent_head;
104:             if let Ok(current_tip) = git::rev_parse(parent) {
105:                 if current_tip != *stored_head {
106:                     ui::info(&format!("Would restack `{branch_name}` onto `{parent}`"));
107:                     any_restack = true;
108:                 }
109:             }
110:         }
111: 
112:         if !any_restack {
113:             ui::info("No restacking needed based on current local state");
114:         }
115: 
116:         ui::hint("Run `ez sync` (without --dry-run) to apply these changes");
117:         return Ok(());
118:     }
119: 
120:     // Autostash: stash before any mutations.
121:     let stashed = if autostash {
122:         let did_stash = git::stash_push()?;
123:         if did_stash {
124:             ui::info("Stashed uncommitted changes (--autostash)");
125:         }
126:         did_stash
127:     } else {
128:         false
129:     };
130: 
131:     let result = run_sync_inner(force);
132: 
133:     if stashed {
134:         if let Err(e) = git::stash_pop() {
135:             ui::warn(&format!("Failed to pop autostash: {e}"));
136:         } else {
137:             ui::info("Restored stashed changes");
138:         }
139:     }
140: 
141:     result
142: }
143: 
144: fn run_sync_inner(force: bool) -> Result<()> {
145:     let mut state = StackState::load()?;
146:     let original_branch = git::current_branch()?;
147:     let original_root = git::repo_root()?;
148:     let mut shell_cd_path: Option<String> = None;
149:     let mut cleaned_current_worktree = false;
150: 
151:     // Fetch from remote.
152:     ui::info(&format!("Fetching from `{}`...", state.remote));
153:     git::fetch(&state.remote)?;
154: 
155:     match git::reset_branch_to_latest_remote(
156:         &state.remote,
157:         &state.trunk,
158:         &original_branch,
159:         &original_root,
160:     ) {
161:         Ok(true) => ui::info(&format!(
162:             "Reset `{}` to latest `{}/{}`",
163:             state.trunk, state.remote, state.trunk
164:         )),
165:         Ok(false) => {}
166:         Err(e) => ui::warn(&format!("Could not update `{}` — {e}", state.trunk)),
167:     }
168: 
169:     // Build branch→worktree map for pruning merged branches.
170:     // Only include worktrees under .worktrees/ — the main worktree must never be removed.
171:     let worktree_map: std::collections::HashMap<String, String> = git::worktree_list()
172:         .unwrap_or_default()
173:         .into_iter()
174:         .filter(|wt| wt.path.contains("/.worktrees/"))
175:         .filter_map(|wt| wt.branch.map(|b| (b, wt.path)))
176:         .collect();
177:     let main_root = git::main_worktree_root().unwrap_or_else(|_| original_root.clone());
178:     let current_dir = std::env::current_dir()
179:         .ok()
180:         .map(|path| path.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
181:         .unwrap_or_default();
182: 
183:     // Detect merged PRs and clean up.
184:     let managed_branches = {
185:         let mut order = state.topo_order();
186:         // Also include branches not in topo order (orphaned branches).
187:         for key in state.branches.keys() {
188:             if !order.contains(key) {
189:                 order.push(key.clone());
190:             }
191:         }
192:         order
193:     };
194:     let local_branches = git::branch_list().unwrap_or_default();
195:     let cleanup_candidates =
196:         cleanup_candidate_branches(&state.trunk, &managed_branches, &local_branches);
197:     let mut cleaned = Vec::new();
198:     let has_any_prs = !cleanup_candidates.is_empty();
199:     let pr_statuses = if has_any_prs {
200:         let sp = ui::spinner("Checking PR states...");
201:         // Collect all unique repos to query: global repo + per-branch pr_repo values.
202:         let mut repos_to_query: Vec<Option<String>> = Vec::new();
203:         let bulk_repo = state.repo.clone().filter(|s| !s.is_empty());
204:         repos_to_query.push(bulk_repo);
205:         let mut seen_repos = std::collections::HashSet::new();
206:         for meta in state.branches.values() {
207:             if let Some(ref repo) = meta.pr_repo {
208:                 if !repo.is_empty() && seen_repos.insert(repo.clone()) {
209:                     repos_to_query.push(Some(repo.clone()));
210:                 }
211:             }
212:         }
213:         // Also add None (default repo from gh) if we haven't queried it.
214:         if repos_to_query.is_empty() {
215:             repos_to_query.push(None);
216:         }
217:         let mut statuses = std::collections::HashMap::new();
218:         for repo in repos_to_query {
219:             let page = github::get_all_pr_statuses_in_repo(repo.as_deref());
220:             for (branch, info) in page {
221:                 statuses.entry(branch).or_insert(info);
222:             }
223:         }
224:         sp.finish_and_clear();
225:         statuses
226:     } else {
227:         std::collections::HashMap::new()
228:     };
229: 
230:     for branch_name in &cleanup_candidates {
231:         let meta = state.get_branch(branch_name).ok().cloned();
232:         let is_managed = meta.is_some();
233:         let pr_info = pr_statuses.get(branch_name.as_str());
234:         let pr_number = meta
235:             .as_ref()
236:             .and_then(|m| m.pr_number)
237:             .or(pr_info.map(|pr| pr.number));
238:         let parent = meta.as_ref().map(|m| m.parent.clone());
239: 
240:         // Auto-clean branches that no longer exist locally (deleted outside of ez).
241:         if !git::branch_exists(branch_name) {
242:             if !is_managed {
243:                 continue;
244:             }
245:             let parent = parent.clone().expect("managed branch should have a parent");
246:             if git::branch_exists(&parent) {
247:                 let _ = state.reparent_children_preserving_parent_head(branch_name, &parent)?;
248:             } else {
249:                 // Parent also deleted — reparent children to trunk, but keep their old base SHA
250:                 // so a later restack still knows what to rebase from.
251:                 let trunk_name = state.trunk.clone();
252:                 let _ = state.reparent_children_preserving_parent_head(branch_name, &trunk_name)?;
253:             }
254:             state.remove_branch(branch_name);
255:             ui::info(&format!("Cleaned up `{branch_name}` (deleted outside ez)"));
256:             cleaned.push(branch_name.clone());
257:             continue;
258:         }
259: 
260:         let merged_via_git = if pr_info.is_none() {
261:             git::is_ancestor(branch_name, &state.trunk)
262:         } else {
263:             false
264:         };
265: 
266:         // Diff-level check: only for branches WITHOUT a PR.
267:         // If a PR exists, the PR status is authoritative. An empty diff might just
268:         // mean someone cherry-picked the changes, not that the PR was merged.
269:         let merged_via_diff =
270:             if is_managed && pr_info.is_none() && !merged_via_git && pr_number.is_none() {
271:                 let range = format!("{}...{}", state.trunk, branch_name);
272:                 git::diff(&range, true, false)
273:                     .map(|stat| stat.trim().is_empty())
274:                     .unwrap_or(false)
275:             } else {
276:                 false
277:             };
278: 
279:         let cleanup_reason = cleanup_reason(pr_info, merged_via_git, merged_via_diff);
280: 
281:         if cleanup_reason.is_none() {
282:             continue;
283:         }
284: 
285:         let cleanup_reason = cleanup_reason.unwrap_or("merged");
286: 
287:         // Remove worktree for this branch (if any) before mutating stack state.
288:         // If cleanup fails, keep the branch tracked so `ez sync --force` or `ez delete`
289:         // can recover it later.
290:         if let Some(wt_path) = worktree_map.get(branch_name.as_str()) {
291:             let is_current_worktree = inside_worktree_path(&current_dir, wt_path)
292:                 || inside_worktree_path(&original_root, wt_path);
293:             if is_current_worktree && let Err(e) = std::env::set_current_dir(&main_root) {
294:                 ui::warn(&format!(
295:                     "Could not move out of worktree `{wt_path}` before cleanup: {e}"
296:                 ));
297:                 ui::info(&format!(
298:                     "Kept `{branch_name}` tracked because cleanup did not complete"
299:                 ));
300:                 ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
301:                     "cmd": "sync",
302:                     "branch": branch_name,
303:                     "action": "cleanup_skipped",
304:                     "reason": "cwd_move_failed",
305:                     "parent": parent,
306:                     "worktree": wt_path,
307:                 }));
308:                 continue;
309:             }
310:             let result = if force {
311:                 git::worktree_remove_force(wt_path)
312:             } else {
313:                 git::worktree_remove(wt_path)
314:             };
315:             match result {
316:                 Ok(()) => {
317:                     ui::info(&format!("Removed worktree at `{wt_path}`"));
318:                     if is_current_worktree {
319:                         shell_cd_path = Some(main_root.clone());
320:                         cleaned_current_worktree = true;
321:                     }
322:                 }
323:                 Err(e) => {
324:                     ui::warn(&format!(
325:                         "Could not remove worktree at `{wt_path}`: {e}\n  Hint: use `ez sync --force` to discard uncommitted changes"
326:                     ));
327:                     ui::info(&format!(
328:                         "Kept `{branch_name}` tracked because cleanup did not complete"
329:                     ));
330:                     ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
331:                         "cmd": "sync",
332:                         "branch": branch_name,
333:                         "action": "cleanup_skipped",
334:                         "reason": "worktree_remove_failed",
335:                         "parent": parent,
336:                         "worktree": wt_path,
337:                     }));
338:                     continue;
339:                 }
340:             }
341:         }
342: 
343:         // If we're on the branch being deleted, switch to trunk first.
344:         if *branch_name == original_branch && !cleaned_current_worktree {
345:             if let Err(e) = git::checkout(&state.trunk) {
346:                 ui::warn(&format!("Could not switch to trunk: {e}"));
347:                 ui::info(&format!(
348:                     "Kept `{branch_name}` tracked because cleanup did not complete"
349:                 ));
350:                 ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
351:                     "cmd": "sync",
352:                     "branch": branch_name,
353:                     "action": "cleanup_skipped",
354:                     "reason": "checkout_failed",
355:                     "parent": parent,
356:                 }));
357:                 continue;
358:             }
359:         }
360: 
361:         // Delete local branch. If this fails, keep the branch tracked so cleanup can be retried.
362:         if git::branch_exists(branch_name)
363:             && let Err(e) = git::delete_branch(branch_name, true)
364:         {
365:             ui::warn(&format!(
366:                 "Could not delete local branch `{branch_name}`: {e}"
367:             ));
368:             ui::info(&format!(
369:                 "Kept `{branch_name}` tracked because cleanup did not complete"
370:             ));
371:             ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
372:                 "cmd": "sync",
373:                 "branch": branch_name,
374:                 "action": "cleanup_skipped",
375:                 "reason": "branch_delete_failed",
376:                 "parent": parent,
377:             }));
378:             continue;
379:         }
380: 
381:         if is_managed {
382:             let parent_name = parent.clone().expect("managed branch should have a parent");
383: 
384:             // Propagate repo metadata to direct children BEFORE removing the branch,
385:             // so children don't lose their effective_pr_repo resolution chain.
386:             let direct_children = state.children_of(branch_name);
387:             let merged_pr_repo = state.effective_pr_repo(branch_name);
388:             let merged_push_remote = Some(state.effective_push_remote(branch_name));
389:             for child in &direct_children {
390:                 let child_meta = state.get_branch_mut(child)?;
391:                 if child_meta.pr_repo.is_none() {
392:                     child_meta.pr_repo = merged_pr_repo.clone();
393:                 }
394:                 if child_meta.push_remote.is_none() {
395:                     child_meta.push_remote = merged_push_remote.clone();
396:                 }
397:             }
398: 
399:             let _ = state.reparent_children_preserving_parent_head(branch_name, &parent_name)?;
400: 
401:             // Update child PR bases after reparenting (cross-fork-aware, no_repoint-guarded).
402:             for child in &direct_children {
403:                 let child_pr = state.get_branch(child).ok().and_then(|m| m.pr_number);
404:                 let Some(child_pr_number) = child_pr else { continue };
405:                 if state.effective_no_repoint(child) {
406:                     ui::warn(&format!(
407:                         "Skipped PR base update for `{child}` (no_repoint is set) \u{2014} update PR #{child_pr_number} manually"
408:                     ));
409:                     continue;
410:                 }
411:                 let child_repo = state.effective_pr_repo(child);
412:                 if let Err(e) = github::update_pr_base_in_repo(
413:                     child_pr_number,
414:                     &parent_name,
415:                     child_repo.as_deref(),
416:                 ) {
417:                     ui::warn(&format!(
418:                         "Could not update PR #{child_pr_number} base for `{child}` to `{parent_name}`: {e}"
419:                     ));
420:                 } else {
421:                     ui::info(&format!(
422:                         "Updated PR #{child_pr_number} base for `{child}` to `{parent_name}`"
423:                     ));
424:                 }
425:             }
426: 
427:             state.remove_branch(branch_name);
428:         }
429: 
430:         let cleanup_label = if cleanup_reason == "pr_closed" {
431:             "PR closed"
432:         } else {
433:             "merged"
434:         };
435:         ui::info(&format!("Cleaned up `{branch_name}` ({cleanup_label})"));
436:         ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
437:             "cmd": "sync",
438:             "branch": branch_name,
439:             "action": "cleaned",
440:             "reason": cleanup_reason,
441:         }));
442:         cleaned.push(branch_name.clone());
443:     }
444: 
445:     // Pre-flight checks: detect merge commits, redundant branches, stale metadata.
446:     let checks = preflight::check_all(&state);
447:     if !checks.is_empty() {
448:         ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
449:             "cmd": "sync",
450:             "action": "preflight",
451:             "summary": preflight::summary(&checks),
452:         }));
453:     }
454:     if preflight::report_and_check(&checks, force) {
455:         bail!(EzError::UserMessage(
456:             "sync aborted — resolve the issues above or use `ez sync --force`".to_string()
457:         ));
458:     }
459: 
460:     // Build a set of fully-redundant branches so we can skip their rebase.
461:     let redundant_branches: std::collections::HashSet<String> = checks
462:         .iter()
463:         .filter(|c| c.all_redundant)
464:         .map(|c| c.branch.clone())
465:         .collect();
466: 
467:     // Restack remaining branches.
468:     let order = state.topo_order();
469:     let mut restacked = 0;
470: 
471:     for branch_name in &order {
472:         let meta = state.get_branch(branch_name)?;
473:         let parent = meta.parent.clone();
474:         let stored_parent_head = meta.parent_head.clone();
475: 
476:         // Skip branches that no longer exist (shouldn't happen after cleanup above, but be safe).
477:         if !git::branch_exists(branch_name) {
478:             continue;
479:         }
480: 
481:         let current_parent_tip = git::rev_parse(&parent)?;
482: 
483:         if current_parent_tip == stored_parent_head {
484:             continue;
485:         }
486: 
487:         // Instead of skipping, detach worktree HEAD so rebase can proceed.
488:         let worktree_path = if let Ok(Some(wt_path)) = git::branch_checked_out_elsewhere(branch_name, &original_root) {
489:             ui::info(&format!("Detaching `{branch_name}` in worktree `{wt_path}` for rebase..."));
490:             git::detach_worktree_head(&wt_path)?;
491:             Some(wt_path)
492:         } else {
493:             None
494:         };
495: 
496:         // If all commits are redundant, skip rebase and just update metadata.
497:         if redundant_branches.contains(branch_name) {
498:             let meta = state.get_branch_mut(branch_name)?;
499:             meta.parent_head = current_parent_tip;
500:             ui::info(&format!(
501:                 "Skipped rebase for `{branch_name}` — all commits already in `{parent}` (metadata updated)"
502:             ));
503:             ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
504:                 "cmd": "sync",
505:                 "branch": branch_name,
506:                 "action": "redundant_skip",
507:                 "parent": parent,
508:             }));
509:             // Reattach worktree if we detached it.
510:             if let Some(ref wt_path) = worktree_path {
511:                 let _ = git::reattach_worktree(wt_path, branch_name);
512:             }
513:             restacked += 1;
514:             continue;
515:         }
516: 
517:         let before_sha = git::rev_parse(branch_name).unwrap_or_default();
518: 
519:         // If merge commits were detected but --force was used, use plain rebase
520:         // (better patch-id skipping) instead of rebase --onto.
521:         let has_merges = checks.iter().any(|c| c.branch == *branch_name && c.merge_commits > 0);
522: 
523:         let sp = ui::spinner(&format!("Restacking `{branch_name}` onto `{parent}`..."));
524:         let outcome = if has_merges {
525:             ui::info(&format!(
526:                 "Branch `{branch_name}` has merge commits — using safe rebase mode"
527:             ));
528:             match git::rebase(&parent, branch_name) {
529:                 Ok(true) => git::RebaseOutcome::RebasingComplete,
530:                 Ok(false) => git::RebaseOutcome::Conflict(git::RebaseConflict {
531:                     conflicting_files: vec![],
532:                     stderr: "rebase conflict during safe rebase mode".to_string(),
533:                 }),
534:                 Err(e) => return Err(e),
535:             }
536:         } else {
537:             git::rebase_onto(&current_parent_tip, &stored_parent_head, branch_name)?
538:         };
539:         sp.finish_and_clear();
540: 
541:         match outcome {
542:             git::RebaseOutcome::RebasingComplete => {
543:                 let meta = state.get_branch_mut(branch_name)?;
544:                 meta.parent_head = current_parent_tip;
545:                 restacked += 1;
546:                 ui::info(&format!("Restacked `{branch_name}` onto `{parent}`"));
547: 
548:                 // Post-restack: detect and auto-drop redundant commits.
549:                 let mut redundant_count: u64 = 0;
550:                 if let Ok(cherry) = git::cherry(&parent, branch_name) {
551:                     let redundant: Vec<&str> =
552:                         cherry.lines().filter(|l| l.starts_with("- ")).collect();
553:                     if !redundant.is_empty() {
554:                         redundant_count = redundant.len() as u64;
555:                         ui::info(&format!(
556:                             "Dropping {redundant_count} redundant commit(s) from `{branch_name}` (already in `{parent}`)",
557:                         ));
558:                         match git::rebase(&parent, branch_name) {
559:                             Ok(true) => {
560:                                 ui::info(&format!(
561:                                     "Dropped redundant commits from `{branch_name}`"
562:                                 ));
563:                             }
564:                             Ok(false) => {
565:                                 ui::warn(&format!(
566:                                     "Could not auto-drop redundant commits from `{branch_name}` (conflict)"
567:                                 ));
568:                                 ui::hint(&format!(
569:                                     "Run `git rebase {parent}` on `{branch_name}` manually and skip redundant commits"
570:                                 ));
571:                             }
572:                             Err(e) => {
573:                                 ui::warn(&format!(
574:                                     "Could not clean up redundant commits from `{branch_name}`: {e}"
575:                                 ));
576:                             }
577:                         }
578:                     }
579:                 }
580: 
581:                 let after_sha = git::rev_parse(branch_name).unwrap_or_default();
582:                 ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
583:                     "cmd": "sync",
584:                     "branch": branch_name,
585:                     "action": "restacked",
586:                     "parent": parent,
587:                     "before": &before_sha[..before_sha.len().min(7)],
588:                     "after": &after_sha[..after_sha.len().min(7)],
589:                     "redundant_commits": redundant_count,
590:                     "safe_rebase_mode": has_merges,
591:                 }));
592: 
593:                 // Reattach worktree if we detached it.
594:                 if let Some(ref wt_path) = worktree_path {
595:                     if !git::reattach_worktree(wt_path, branch_name)? {
596:                         ui::warn(&format!(
597:                             "Could not reattach `{branch_name}` in worktree `{wt_path}` — \
598:                              worktree may have dirty files that conflict with rebased commits.\n  \
599:                              Run `cd {wt_path} && git checkout {branch_name}` to reattach manually."
600:                         ));
601:                     }
602:                 }
603:             }
604:             git::RebaseOutcome::Conflict(conflict) => {
605:                 if let Some(ref wt_path) = worktree_path {
606:                     let _ = git::reattach_worktree(wt_path, branch_name);
607:                 }
608:                 // Save progress so the user can fix and continue.
609:                 state.save()?;
610:                 rebase_conflict::report("sync", branch_name, &parent, &conflict, "ez restack");
611:                 bail!(EzError::RebaseConflict(branch_name.clone()));
612:             }
613:         }
614:     }
615: 
616:     state.save()?;
617: 
618:     // Return to original branch if it still exists.
619:     // If it was cleaned up (merged), fall back to trunk — but trunk might be in another worktree.
620:     if cleaned_current_worktree {
621:         ui::info(&format!(
622:             "Current worktree `{original_branch}` was cleaned up — switched context to repo root"
623:         ));
624:     } else if git::branch_exists(&original_branch) {
625:         let _ = git::checkout(&original_branch);
626:     } else {
627:         match git::checkout(&state.trunk) {
628:             Ok(()) => ui::info(&format!(
629:                 "Previous branch `{original_branch}` was cleaned up — switched to `{}`",
630:                 state.trunk
631:             )),
632:             Err(_) => ui::warn(&format!(
633:                 "Previous branch `{original_branch}` was cleaned up. Switch to another branch manually \
634:                  (trunk may be checked out in another worktree)."
635:             )),
636:         }
637:     }
638: 
639:     if cleaned.is_empty() && restacked == 0 {
640:         ui::info("Everything is up to date");
641:     } else {
642:         ui::success(&format!(
643:             "Synced ({} cleaned, {} restacked)",
644:             cleaned.len(),
645:             restacked
646:         ));
647:     }
648: 
649:     // Prune stale worktree admin entries.
650:     let _ = git::worktree_prune();
651: 
652:     if let Some(path) = shell_cd_path {
653:         println!("{path}");
654:     }
655: 
656:     Ok(())
657: }
658: 
659: #[cfg(test)]
660: mod tests {
661:     use super::*;
662: 
663:     #[test]
664:     fn run_signatures_compile() {
665:         // Verifies the public API is correct at compile time.
666:         let f: fn(bool, bool, bool) -> anyhow::Result<()> = super::run;
667:         let _ = std::mem::size_of_val(&f);
668:     }
669: 
670:     #[test]
671:     fn cleanup_candidate_branches_includes_local_unmanaged_branches() {
672:         let managed = vec!["feat/a".to_string()];
673:         let local = vec![
674:             "main".to_string(),
675:             "feat/a".to_string(),
676:             "feat/b".to_string(),
677:             "feat/c".to_string(),
678:         ];
679: 
680:         assert_eq!(
681:             cleanup_candidate_branches("main", &managed, &local),
682:             vec![
683:                 "feat/a".to_string(),
684:                 "feat/b".to_string(),
685:                 "feat/c".to_string()
686:             ]
687:         );
688:     }
689: 
690:     #[test]
691:     fn cleanup_reason_prefers_pr_state() {
692:         let closed = github::PrInfo {
693:             number: 42,
694:             url: String::new(),
695:             state: "CLOSED".to_string(),
696:             title: String::new(),
697:             base: "main".to_string(),
698:             is_draft: false,
699:             merged: false,
700:         };
701:         let merged = github::PrInfo {
702:             merged: true,
703:             ..closed.clone()
704:         };
705: 
706:         assert_eq!(cleanup_reason(Some(&merged), false, false), Some("merged"));
707:         assert_eq!(
708:             cleanup_reason(Some(&closed), false, false),
709:             Some("pr_closed")
710:         );
711:         assert_eq!(
712:             cleanup_reason(
713:                 Some(&github::PrInfo {
714:                     state: "OPEN".to_string(),
715:                     ..closed
716:                 }),
717:                 true,
718:                 true
719:             ),
720:             None
721:         );
722:         assert_eq!(cleanup_reason(None, true, false), Some("merged"));
723:         assert_eq!(cleanup_reason(None, false, true), Some("merged"));
724:         assert_eq!(cleanup_reason(None, false, false), None);
725:     }
726: 
727:     #[test]
728:     fn inside_worktree_path_matches_nested_paths_only() {
729:         assert!(inside_worktree_path(
730:             "/repo/.worktrees/feat-a",
731:             "/repo/.worktrees/feat-a"
732:         ));
733:         assert!(inside_worktree_path(
734:             "/repo/.worktrees/feat-a/src/components",
735:             "/repo/.worktrees/feat-a"
736:         ));
737:         assert!(!inside_worktree_path(
738:             "/repo/.worktrees/feat-ab",
739:             "/repo/.worktrees/feat-a"
740:         ));
741:     }
742: }

Instructions from: /Users/drewry.pope/ez-stack/.worktrees/feat-repoint-after-merge/AGENTS.md
# AGENTS.md

Instructions for AI agents working with `ez-stack`.

## Purpose

`ez` is a CLI for managing stacked pull requests on GitHub. If a repository has `.git/ez/stack.json`, branch management and PR operations should go through `ez`, not raw `git checkout -b`, `git commit`, `git push`, or `gh pr create`.

## Install or update the tool

```bash
# First install
pip install ez-stack
ez setup --yes    # configures shell integration (PATH + worktree auto-cd)

# Update to latest (auto-detects install method)
ez update

# Check for updates without installing
ez update --check
```

Requirements:

- `git`
- `gh`, authenticated via `gh auth login`
- Rust/Cargo available to install `ez-stack`

## Install the skill

Install the ez-workflow skill into the current repo so all agents auto-discover it:

```bash
ez skill install
# Installs canonical skill to .agents/skills/ez-workflow/SKILL.md
# Symlinks agent-specific skill roots like .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/
# Commit the file so other agents in this repo use it too
```

This installs the workflow defined in [`SKILL.md`](./SKILL.md).

## Core rule

When `.git/ez/stack.json` exists, prefer these commands:

- Create branch: `ez create <name>`
- Commit specific files: `ez commit -m "msg" -- path1 path2`
- Commit all tracked changes: `ez commit -am "msg"`
- Commit tracked + untracked changes: `ez commit -Am "msg"`
- Commit patch-selected hunks: `git add -p` then `ez commit -m "msg"`
- Diff vs parent: `ez diff --stat` or `ez diff --name-only`
- Get parent branch: `ez parent`
- Push current branch and create/update PR: `ez push`
- Push the stack: `ez submit`
- Sync after trunk changes or merges: `ez sync` or `ez sync --autostash`
- Inspect state programmatically: `ez status --json` and `ez log --json`

Avoid these raw commands in an `ez`-managed repo:

- `git checkout -b ...` → `ez create`
- `git commit -m ...` → `ez commit -m`
- `git push` → `ez push`
- `gh pr create` → `ez push`
- `git diff main...HEAD` → `ez diff`

## Output format

Every command appends a status line to stderr: `[ok | 45ms]` or `[exit:3 | 120ms]`.
Use this to branch on exit status and learn command cost.

Discovery: `ez` (no args) lists all commands (exit 0). `ez <command> --help` shows full details.

## Good agent patterns

```bash
# Check whether the repo is managed by ez
test -f .git/ez/stack.json && echo "ez-managed"

# Create from a specific base branch
ez create feat/my-change --from main

# Preferred: stage specific files and commit in one step
ez commit -m "fix: update parser" -- src/parser.rs

# Bulk update: stage tracked files and commit in one step
ez commit -am "chore: update generated fixtures"

# Bulk update including new untracked files
ez commit -Am "feat: add generated fixtures and new docs"

# Partial hunk selection stays in git
git add -p
ez commit -m "fix: keep only intended hunks"

# Multi-paragraph commit message
ez commit -m "feat: add parser" -m "Implements recursive descent parsing."

# Commit only if there are staged changes
ez commit -m "chore: update" --if-changed

# Self-review before push
ez diff --stat

# Get parent branch name for scripting
ez parent

# Sync safely with a dirty working tree
ez sync --autostash

# Read current stack state as JSON
ez status --json
ez log --json
```

## Exit codes

- `0`: success
- `1`: unexpected error
- `2`: GitHub / `gh` error
- `3`: rebase conflict
- `4`: stale remote ref
- `5`: usage error
- `6`: unstaged changes

## References

- [`README.md`](./README.md): user-facing overview and command reference
- [`SKILL.md`](./SKILL.md): full skill instructions for skill-compatible agents
- [`CLAUDE.md`](./CLAUDE.md): project context and design principles

</system-reminder>
